data.title: Only a third of Australians think Trump's presidency would be good for cryptocurrencies
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News After Trump was elected as the US President, the cryptocurrency market soared due to his promise to support the industry, but a recent survey shows that only about a third of Australians think he is optimistic about cryptocurrencies.
A survey of 2,100 local adults released by Australian cryptocurrency exchange Independent Reserve on February 21 found that 31% of people think Trump is good for cryptocurrencies, while 8% think he is bad for the industry. Most respondents (about 60%) are neutral on the topic.
The survey found that cryptocurrency investors have a more positive view of Trump than non-investors. Half of Australian cryptocurrency investors said he is positive about cryptocurrencies, while 44% are neutral. Only 6% said he is negative about the cryptocurrency industry. Among non-cryptocurrency investors, only about 20% said he is positive about cryptocurrencies, while 10% think he is negative for the industry. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Japan's core inflation hits 19-month high, reinforcing expectations of a rate hike by the central bank
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Data released earlier today showed that Japan's core consumer inflation accelerated in January to 3.2% year-on-year, the fastest growth in 19 months. The stronger-than-expected growth reinforced expectations that the Bank of Japan may continue to raise interest rates as inflationary pressures persist. The price increase appears to be in line with the central bank's forecast. Analysts pointed out that service industry inflation did not accelerate, rising 1.4% year-on-year in January, compared with 1.6% in December, but overall, commodity inflation did not slow down. Japan's inflation rate has been higher than the central bank's 2% target for nearly three years. This has prompted a shift in monetary policy. Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda previously hinted that the central bank may raise interest rates further if wage growth continues to support consumption, enabling companies to raise wages and keep prices rising. (Jinshi)

data.title: Japan's Core Consumer Inflation Reaches 19-Month High In January
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Japan's core consumer inflation accelerated in January, reaching 3.2% year-on-year, marking the fastest pace in 19 months. This stronger-than-expected growth has reinforced expectations that the Bank of Japan may continue to raise interest rates as inflationary pressures persist. The rise in prices aligns with the central bank's forecasts. Analysts noted that while inflation in the services sector did not accelerate, increasing by 1.4% year-on-year in January compared to 1.6% in December, overall goods inflation has not slowed. For nearly three years, Japan's inflation rate has remained above the central bank's 2% target, prompting a shift in monetary policy. Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda previously indicated that if wage growth continues to support consumption, enabling companies to raise wages and maintain price increases, the central bank might further increase interest rates.

data.title: LBank will list Infinite Money Glitch (IMG)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, LBank will launch Infinite Money Glitch (IMG) at 10:40 (SGT) on February 21, and open the IMG/USDT trading pair. The deposit service will be opened at 10:30 on February 21, and the withdrawal service will be opened at 11:00 on February 22. 10:17

data.title: A whale address has withdrawn 4,700 ETH from CEX
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale address that had been silent for about 500 days woke up 7 hours ago and withdrew a total of 4,700 ETH (worth approximately US$12.98 million) from Coinbase and Binance.

data.title: Whale Resumes Virtual Purchases After Significant Loss
data.description: According to Odaily, on-chain data analyst Yu Jin has reported that a whale, who recently incurred a loss of $5.02 million (-36%) on VIRTUAL earlier this month, has resumed purchasing the cryptocurrency. Three hours ago, the whale transferred 4,006 ETH, equivalent to approximately $10.9 million, to a new address. From this amount, 419 ETH, or about $1.14 million, has been spent to acquire 1.049 million VIRTUAL tokens at an average price of $1.09 each. Previously, this address had invested $13.91 million to buy 5.038 million VIRTUAL tokens at an average price of $2.76 each. The whale later sold at $1.76 per token, resulting in a $5.02 million loss.

data.title: BitMart launches contract simulation trading, allowing you to experience real trading with zero risk
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News BitMart announced the launch of the "Contract Simulation Trading" function, which is committed to providing users with a risk-free trading practice platform. Without the need for real capital costs, users can experience highly simulated contract trading through this function and improve their trading skills in market changes. At the same time, the platform provides a wealth of USDX simulated asset support to help users become familiar with complex trading mechanisms in a relaxed environment. The contract simulation trading function is now available on the BitMart official website and App.

data.title: Two early LDO investors sold 2.9 million LDOs
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Spot on Chain monitoring, F2Pool co-founder Wang Chun sold 300,000 LDOs for $518,000 about 10 hours ago. Since the beginning of 2025, he has sold 5.8 million LDOs (worth about $10.27 million) at an average price of $1.771, and now still holds 2.2 million LDOs (worth about $3.81 million).
Another investor, Wolfgang Albrecht, transferred 2.6 million LDOs (worth about $4.51 million) to Binance 16 hours ago. The investor received an unlocking quota of 3.33 million LDOs two years ago and has currently sold 3.28 million LDOs at an average price of about $1.895, with a remaining position of 50,000 LDOs.

data.title: Trader Ansem sold 215,000 TAIKO airdrops and missed out on $115,000 in profits
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, trader Ansem received an airdrop of 215,113 KAITO and sold it for 76 ETH (worth about $206,000). The 215,113 KAITOs he sold are currently worth about $320,000.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees Significant Daily Gains And Losses
data.description:
According to PANews, several cryptocurrencies have experienced notable fluctuations in their daily trading values. Leading the gains, MKR is currently priced at $1,434.5, marking a 16.18% increase. OM follows with a price of $8.0453, up by 9.23%. TIA is trading at $3.75, reflecting an 8.26% rise, while APE is at $0.712, with a 6.14% increase. WIF rounds out the top gainers at $0.64, up by 5.96%.

Conversely, some tokens have seen declines. GLM is priced at $0.314, experiencing a 1.13% decrease. ILV is trading at $18.85, down by 0.48%. XRP's value has decreased by 0.31%, now at $2.664. XLM is priced at $0.341, reflecting a 0.21% drop, and TON is at $3.571, with a slight decrease of 0.08%.
